capacity_evidence.probe_command: TT_LOGGER_LEVEL=fatal MISTRAL_SMALL_24B_CONTEXT_PROBE_LEN=<length> TT_VISIBLE_DEVICES=2,3 pytest -q -s models/autoports/mistralai_mistral_small_24b_instruct_2501/tests/test_context_capacity.py (add MISTRAL_SMALL_24B_CONTEXT_EXPECT_OOM=1 for the first failing length)
capacity_evidence.device: Blackhole p300c UMD, one logical 1x1 mesh device
capacity_evidence.probe_process_policy: one length per fresh process on a one-device mesh; full-shape synthetic BF16 weights have the same device allocation sizes as real weights; output is copied to host to force completion

first_failure.operation_class: device DRAM tensor allocation during prefill

capacity_evidence.post_failure_health_check: tt-smi -ls --local returned all four Blackhole chips after normal fixture teardown
capacity_evidence.explanation: At emitted batch 32, the current dense BF16 path completes at logical sequence length 3583 and the immediately adjacent length 3584 reproducibly fails a physical DRAM allocation, establishing the exact current boundary. HF context 32768 is also impossible for this path: one [32,32768,32768] BF16 MLP activation alone is 68,719,476,736 bytes, exceeding the measured 34,178,731,008 device bytes before weights, input, KV cache, or other live tensors.

optimized_decoder_capacity_evidence.probe_command: TT_VISIBLE_DEVICES=2,3 MISTRAL_SMALL_24B_OPT_CONTEXT_PROBE_LEN=<length> pytest -q -s models/autoports/mistralai_mistral_small_24b_instruct_2501/tests/test_optimized_decoder.py::test_optimized_batch32_context_capacity_probe (add MISTRAL_SMALL_24B_OPT_CONTEXT_EXPECT_OOM=1 only for an expected OOM)
optimized_decoder_capacity_evidence.device: Blackhole p300c UMD, one logical 1x1 mesh device
optimized_decoder_capacity_evidence.selected_policy: BFP4 LoFi attention and MLP weights, BFP8 KV cache, BF16 activations, flattened token matrix, and 576-token internal MLP chunks
optimized_decoder_capacity_evidence.probe_process_policy: one length per fresh process; full output copied to host to force device completion
optimized_decoder_capacity_evidence.adaptation_evidence: The first 3584-token attempt failed an explicit QKV program config at 33,018,624 bytes of static circular buffers versus 1,572,864 bytes L1, not DRAM capacity. Restricting the measured large-prefill configs to at most 576 flattened tokens and using TTNN's general large-M config above that envelope made 3584 and 4096 pass. The public sequence length remains unrestricted; MLP internals chunk only above 576 flattened tokens.
optimized_decoder_capacity_evidence.claim: 4096 is a tested supported optimized-decoder length, not a measured hardware maximum. The earlier functional 3583/3584 boundary remains valid only for the BF16 functional implementation.

ir_provenance.collective_policy: TP shards and all-reduces collapsed to equivalent dense single-device math over full unsharded Hugging Face weights

notes: The functional BF16 evidence and its exact 3583-token boundary are retained for provenance. The optimized-decoder stage raises the tested contract to 4096 without claiming an unmeasured maximum. This file does not claim multichip, full-model, or serving readiness.
